faq.title: Pricing FAQ
faq.description: Direct answers about plans, credits, renewals, and what you are actually paying for on {{app_domain}}.
faq.tip: Need help choosing a plan? Email {{support_email}}.

items.question: What am I paying for on {{app_domain}}?
items.answer: You are paying for this site’s hosted AI workspace: accounts, credits, saved task history, billing, and support. The browser tools remain separate and free in the browser.

items.question: Do I need a paid plan to use mogged?
items.answer: No. You can browse the public site and use the browser tools without a paid plan. Paid plans matter when you need ongoing credits, saved account workflows, or higher-volume generation.

items.question: Which plan should I start with?
items.answer: Start with the smallest plan while you validate prompts, source files, and workflow fit. Upgrade only after you understand your real monthly usage.

items.question: Do free browser tools consume credits?
items.answer: No. The browser tools run separately and do not use hosted generation credits.

items.question: Do credits pay for mog battles and leaderboard features?
items.answer: Yes. The same balance can be used for hosted mog battles, saved rating history, leaderboard progression, and profile prep. Exact usage depends on the battle mode, scoring route, and settings.

items.question: When do credits expire, and what happens if a failed task is refunded after that window?
items.answer: Credit expiry depends on the source: paid plan credits follow the validity shown on the plan, while free signup or daily claim credits can use shorter reward windows. If a task fails, we restore usable credits, and when the original credits had an expiry window the refund keeps the remaining validity that was left when those credits were spent.

items.question: How do battle credit costs change?
items.answer: The task cost is shown before a paid action. It can change with the battle mode, scoring route, profile prep settings, or leaderboard-related feature you choose.

items.question: Do prompt translation and auto-optimize consume credits?
items.answer: Yes. Translate to English costs 1 credit per successful request, and Auto-optimize costs 2 credits per successful request. If no usable result is returned, we do not deduct credits for that attempt.

items.question: Can I cancel a subscription anytime?
items.answer: Yes. Cancellation stops future renewals, and access continues through the current paid period unless a refund is separately approved under the refund policy or required by law.

items.question: How do I get help with billing or plan selection?
items.answer: Email {{support_email}} if you need help comparing plans, checking credits, or fixing a billing issue.

schema.product_disclaimer: This price describes a {{app_domain}}-hosted service, not official upstream vendor billing.
